Bitcoin's supply is mathematically capped at 21 million coins forever. No CEO, government, or Fed can change it. The last coin will be mined around 2140.
In 2010, Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas worth $41. Those 10,000 BTC are worth $680M today.
The US dollar has lost 97% of its purchasing power since the Federal Reserve was created in 1913. Bitcoin has a fixed supply that cannot be inflated.
Bitcoin's 4th halving occurred in April 2024. Block reward dropped from 6.25 to 3.125 BTC. New supply cut in half by code, not committee.

entries.deal: Gold vs Bitcoin Store of Value
entries.verified_fact: Gold has been mined for 5,000 years and humans keep finding more. Bitcoin's supply is provably fixed at 21M. One is hard money. One just feels like it.
US inflation hit 9.1% in June 2022 — highest since 1981. A $1M contract signed in 2020 had $820K of real purchasing power by 2022. Inflation is a tax nobody voted for.
El Salvador became the first country to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender in September 2021. President Bukele bought 2,381 BTC for the national treasury.
Ja Morant signed his $8.9M rookie deal in 2019 when BTC was $7,200. 10% allocation = 124 BTC.
Evander Holyfield earned over $250M in his boxing career and lost his mansion to foreclosure in 2012. $25M in Bitcoin at $13 would be 1.9M BTC worth $130B today.
Alex Rodriguez signed a $252M deal with the Rangers in 2000 — largest in sports history at the time. Bitcoin didn't exist. That contract is worth $480M in today's dollars due to inflation alone.
Derrick Coleman earned $91M in his NBA career and filed for bankruptcy in 2010. Bitcoin was $0.06 that year. $9.1M would have bought 151M BTC. Do the math.
Trevor Lawrence signed a $36.8M rookie deal in 2021 when BTC was $55,000. 10% allocation = 67 BTC worth $4.5M today on a $3.68M investment.
Joe Lacob bought the Golden State Warriors for $450M in 2010 when BTC was $0.06. The team is now worth $7.7B. $45M in Bitcoin that year would be worth $500B. Different kind of asset.
Lawrence Taylor earned roughly $50M in his NFL career and faced repeated financial and legal problems after retirement. One of the greatest to ever play. One of the worst at keeping money.
Bryce Harper signed a $330M deal with the Phillies in 2019 when BTC was $3,500. 10% = 9,428 BTC worth $638M today. His contract is still paying out. Bitcoin already won.

From 2013 to 2023, Bitcoin returned over 15,000%. The S&P 500 returned roughly 230% in the same period. Every financial advisor who said 'too risky' was technically right about volatility and completely wrong about outcomes.
The average lifespan of a fiat currency is 27 years. The US dollar has survived longer by continuously losing value. Bitcoin has a fixed supply that cannot be inflated.
Bitcoin is secured by over 17,000 nodes globally. No single government, company, or person controls it. Every other asset you own has a counterparty that can freeze, confiscate, or inflate it.
In 1971 Nixon ended the dollar's gold convertibility. Since then the dollar has lost 85% of its value. Every currency in the world became backed by nothing but government promises that same day.

Bitcoin uses roughly 0.5% of global energy consumption. The traditional banking system uses an estimated 263 TWh per year. Bitcoin uses about 127 TWh. The incumbent uses more energy and inflates your savings.

Satoshi Nakamoto mined approximately 1.1 million BTC in Bitcoin's early days and never moved them. At today's prices that's $74B sitting untouched. The creator of the hardest money ever invented never sold.

BlackRock's Bitcoin ETF (IBIT) accumulated $17B in assets in its first 50 days — the fastest ETF launch in history. The world's largest asset manager chose Bitcoin over every other asset class for its first crypto product.
The US government holds approximately 200,000 BTC seized from criminals — worth $13.5B. The government that calls Bitcoin risky is one of its largest holders.
There are only about 88,000 Bitcoin wallets holding 1 BTC or more. There are 22 million millionaires in the US alone. The math on scarcity hasn't fully hit yet.
Bitcoin crashed 50% in March 2020 with stocks during COVID panic. By December it hit all-time highs while the economy struggled. Short-term it trades like risk. Long-term it trades like hard money.
Joe Burrow signed a $275.0M contract in 2023 when BTC was $27,000. 5% allocation = 509 BTC.
In 2016, Venezuela's inflation rate reached 180%
Here are some verified facts about NCAA athlete NIL deals for 2024 and 2025:

Tom Brady signed a $3.3M rookie contract in 2000
In 2008, Zimbabwe's monthly inflation rate peaked at 79,600,000,000% in November
In 2021, Tesla purchased $1.5 billion worth of Bitcoin

The entire 1992 US Olympic Dream Team — Jordan, Magic, Bird, Barkley, Pippen — earned a combined $26M that year. That same $26M in Bitcoin at 2013 prices would be worth over $180M today. The greatest team ever assembled got paid in melting ice cubes.

The NFL minimum salary in 1970 was $9,000. In 2026 it's $795,000. Sounds like progress. But $9,000 in 1970 had more purchasing power than $180,000 today. The dollar lost 95% of its value. Bitcoin was designed so this math never happens again.

Shohei Ohtani signed a $700M deal but deferred $680M — he won't collect most of it until 2034. His advisors trusted the Dodgers would still exist and the dollar would hold value for 10 years. Bitcoin needs neither assumption. It just holds.

Bitcoin's supply cuts in half every 4 years — by design. Most athletes have 4-year windows to earn life-changing money. The last 3 halving cycles produced 10x, 20x, and 15x returns. Two timelines built for each other. Most athletes never connect the dots.

There will only ever be 21 million Bitcoin. The NFL has 1,696 active roster spots. The NBA has 450. There are more players in professional sports than there will ever be whole bitcoins. Most athletes will never own even one. The ones who figure this out early win twice.

Before NIL, college athletes generated billions for their schools and got paid zero. Bitcoin was created for people who do real work and watch someone else capture the value. Sound money doesn't extract — it preserves. Every athlete who went unpaid should understand what that system was.

The average professional sports career is 3.5 years. Bitcoin has been running without interruption for 16 years — longer than most athletes' entire careers. It has never been hacked, shut down, or inflated. Most athletes retire before they understand what they had access to.

The NFL pension pays retired players as little as $560/month for a 3-year career. That amount has lost 70% of its purchasing power since 1980. The players who built the league are being quietly robbed by inflation every single month. Bitcoin fixes the pension problem — permanently.

The Dallas Cowboys were worth $800M in 2000. Today they're worth $10B — a 12x return in 24 years. Bitcoin returned over 10,000x in the same window. Franchise ownership is the smart money play everyone talks about. Bitcoin is the one nobody talks about.
In 2021, Tesla purchased Bitcoin for $1.50 billion
Saquon Barkley signed a 2-year $41.2M extension in March 2025, making him the NFL's first $20M+ per year running back. At current BTC price ($70,434), 10% allocation = 58 BTC.
Josh Allen signed a 6-year $330M extension in March 2025 with $250M guaranteed — an NFL record for guaranteed money at signing. At BTC $70,434, 10% allocation = 468 BTC.
Ja'Marr Chase signed a 4-year $161M extension in March 2025, making him the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history with $112M guaranteed. 10% BTC allocation = 229 BTC.
Tee Higgins signed a 4-year $115M extension with the Bengals in March 2025, keeping the Chase-Higgins duo together through 2028. 10% BTC allocation = 163 BTC.

entries.deal: BTC March 2025 High Contract Purchasing Power
entries.verified_fact: Bitcoin hit $73,079 on March 13, 2025. At that price, Saquon Barkley's $41.2M extension would buy 564 BTC. Josh Allen's $330M would buy 4,516 BTC. Same contracts, same work, different purchasing power.

entries.deal: RB Market Reset vs BTC Appreciation
entries.verified_fact: Saquon Barkley reset the RB market at $20.6M/year. The previous high was Christian McCaffrey at $19M. Meanwhile, Bitcoin has gained 168% since the last RB market reset in 2023. Scarce assets appreciate. Labor gets squeezed.

Josh Allen got $250M guaranteed — an NFL record. But over 6 years, inflation will eat 15-20% of that purchasing power. Bitcoin's 21M supply is guaranteed by math, not lawyers. One guarantee is written in code. The other in paper.

entries.deal: Non-QB Market vs Bitcoin Wallet Scarcity
entries.verified_fact: Ja'Marr Chase is now the highest-paid non-QB at $40.25M/year. There are only 88,000 Bitcoin wallets holding 1+ BTC. There are likely more pro athletes earning $10M+/year than there are whole bitcoins available for them.

Both Ja'Marr Chase and Tee Higgins signed 4-year deals. Bitcoin's halving cycle is also 4 years. By the time their contracts expire in 2028, BTC will have had another supply shock. The 2028 halving price is anyone's guess. The scarcity is not.
In 1971, President Nixon suspended US dollar convertibility to gold at $35 per ounce on August 15

BTC ETFs -\$306M outflows Mar 18-20, 2026 (IBIT -\$46M lead). = 30x avg NFL rookie bonus. Institutions treat billions as noise—athletes' lump sums expire in fiat.
